query:
Now on this feature... Unimatrix came up with this recommendation.  can you find evidence of this being an actual problem, is this the proper root cause? - Batch field additions before compiling — high compile cycle counts
     typically indicate iterative per-field struct changes or cascading type
     errors; complete type definitions and resolve compiler errors in-memory
     before each build

entries.title: Batch structural changes before compiling — multi-file struct extension and bugfixes both benefit from complete-then-compile discipline
Single-file annotation iteration drives compile cycles: batch all field attributes before first build
Large file compile-cycle amplification: search.rs at 4000+ lines produces 94 compile cycles when adding a new pipeline step
Complete all struct/field definition changes before first compile in multi-type iterative fixes
